page: The Microsoft Azure B32s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine instance belonging to the Bsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports hyper-threading with two threads per core and includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512_VNNI) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1895.88 and a multi-core score of 31058.43. Memory performance is a notable strength, with a PassMark memory score of 2828.58, placing it in the top 25% of tested configurations. Large block memory read bandwidth reaches 143.43 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is measured at 63.5 GB/sec. In language model inference tests, the server demonstrated strong performance for text generation using a small 135M model at 290.9 tokens per second, though performance drops to a weak 6.61 tokens per second for prompt processing with a large 70B model.

As a burstable instance, the B32s_v2 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads with variable CPU utilization. The burstable CPU allocation model allows users to pay a lower baseline rate while retaining the ability to scale up to full CPU performance when demand spikes, making it more economical than non-burstable instances with equivalent core counts. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on external network-attached storage, and the burstable CPU model is a tradeoff that makes it unsuitable for sustained, high-utilization compute workloads. It is best suited for development environments, build servers, low-to-medium traffic web applications, and database operations with intermittent traffic patterns.
description: The Microsoft Azure B32s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bsv2 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Operating at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 instance includes 128.0 GB of system memory but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. It features a burstable CPU allocation model, offering qualitative cost advantages for workloads with variable resource demands. Benchmarks show average compute performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 31058.43, alongside strong memory performance, highlighted by a PassMark memory score in the top 25%. While it handles small-model LLM text generation efficiently at 290.9 tokens per second, it struggles with larger models. The B32s_v2 is suited for development environments, build servers, and applications with intermittent traffic patterns that benefit from burstable CPU resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B32s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM. It offers strong memory performance and cost-efficient scaling for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B32s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, designed for workloads with variable CPU demands.
tagline: A burstable 32-vCPU virtual machine with 128 GB of RAM, offering cost-effective scaling for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
Burstable CPU allocation model for cost-efficient handling of variable workloads
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarked systems
No local storage, requiring external network-attached storage solutions
Best suited for development environments, build servers, and intermittent databases
